author: Dr. James Tran
content:
EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT NOTE — 3/8/2023, 7:20 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (Age 19)
Pay status: Self-pay; emergency Medicaid initiated at registration.
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter
Triage acuity: ESI 4

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Left ankle pain after injury at work.

HPI:
19-year-old female who twisted her left ankle approximately 2 hours ago while cleaning a hotel room — stepped on a slick wet floor while carrying linens, ankle inverted. Sudden sharp pain over lateral ankle. Heard a 'pop.' Immediate swelling. Unable to bear weight. Co-worker drove her to ED.

Denies head injury, neck pain, other joint involvement. No prior ankle injuries. No bleeding disorder. Last meal 6 hours ago.

VITALS: T 98.4 F, HR 92, BP 118/76, RR 16, SpO2 100% RA. Pain 7/10 in left ankle.

EXAM:
LEFT ANKLE: Significant lateral swelling and ecchymosis over the lateral malleolus. Tenderness over the anterior talofibular ligament and calcaneofibular ligament. No tenderness over the medial malleolus, base of the 5th metatarsal, navicular, or proximal fibula (Ottawa rules: negative for high suspicion of fracture). Range of motion limited by pain. Unable to bear weight independently. Sensation intact in foot. Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses 2+. Capillary refill brisk.
No other injuries identified on secondary survey.

IMAGING:
LEFT ANKLE X-RAY (3 views): No fracture identified. Lateral soft tissue swelling. Joint spaces preserved. (Read by ED radiologist Dr. Patel.)

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
Left ankle sprain — likely Grade II inversion injury involving the lateral ligament complex. No fracture.

1. RICE protocol: rest, ice, compression with Ace wrap, elevation.
2. Air-cast / lace-up ankle brace provided.
3. Crutches provided; non-weight-bearing as tolerated x 48-72 hours, then progressive weight-bearing.
4. Ibuprofen 600 mg PO q6h PRN x 5 days (with food).
5. Work note: limited duty (no standing, no lifting) for 7-10 days.
6. RTC if no improvement in 1 week or worsening pain, fever, numbness, color change.

ED time: 90 min (including imaging wait).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. James Tran, MD
Department of Emergency Medicine, Johns Hopkins Hospital
Date: 3/8/2023
